reasoning: The FRED tool generated a primary statistical anchor of 0.62 for a short-term increase over the local low of 4.38. We lean on this for the shortest horizon (Nov 2) but adjust it slightly downwards to account for standard macroeconomic drift (the Fed is in an easing cycle as seen from rate cut expectations, meaning general downside pressure on bond yields). Per the instructions, uncertainty must increase as the forecast horizon extends, so probability inputs for later dates represent a gradual reversion toward the maximally uninformed prior of 0.5. Thus, probabilities are 0.58, 0.54, and 0.52 for the three respective dates.
